dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 4 responding for a reported cardiac arrest in the city of Richmond.
Hi, I'm Brian. We've been dispatched for a reported cardiac arrest at --Address Redacted-. Could you confirm the patient's current status?
We responded to a cardiac arrest call. The patient was found unresponsive in bed by facility staff who initiated CPR and BVM ventilations.
Is the patient currently being resuscitated with manual compressions and ventilations?
Yes. They used an AED but it did not recommend shocks. The patient is pulseless and apneic.
Firefighters, is the patient responding to any verbal or painful stimuli?
No response to verbal or physical stimuli. GCS is 3.
Are you ready to take over airway management? We'll need a direct laryngoscope and ETT.
Handing over BVM. McGrath scope is ready for use.
Visualizing vocal cords. ETT inserted to 23cm at the lips. Capnography showing good waveform.
Bilateral breath sounds confirmed. ETT is secured with tube holder and x-collar.
Respiratory rate observed at 12 per minute using ETT. Capnography remains stable during ventilations.
No pulse detected manually. EKG reveals regular electrical activity with no palpable pulses. PEA suspected.
Let's prepare for IO access to support medication administration. Please document baseline vitals.
No pulse detected. Preparing for non-invasive systolic assessment with manual effort unlikely to yield results.
Respiration rate is 12/min via ventilations from ETT. No spontaneous efforts observed.
No systolic or diastolic pressure reported. Blood pressure is 0/0.
Blood glucose could not be obtained. CPR initiated before testing was feasible-documented in EPCR as 0.
Oxygen saturation is not measurable at this stage, recorded per EPCR as 0.
EKG rhythm currently noted as asystolic per monitor findings. No organized rhythm identified at this moment.
Do we have a confirmed medical history for the patient prior to arrest?.
Medical history available: CV-Primary hypertension. No additional illness detailed in documentation.
Any known allergies or medications contributing to presentation?.
No listed allergies or current medications. Unable to confirm at this time per staff report in EPCR.
What precipitated this event according to staff or documentation?
Reported unresponsive by facility staff around 22:27. No previous complaints on file related to arrest timeline.
Given current unresponsiveness, can we assume no pain specific to this event at this time?.
Yes. Pain level is noted as 0, consistent with unresponsive state in EPCR documentation.
What skin findings, if any, were noted by responders upon arrival?.
Slightly pale and cool. No definitive death signs like trismus or dependent lividity observed according to EPCR.
Any trauma or abnormalities in head, neck, or torso noted in assessment?.
No evidence of trauma, bite marks, or lacerations in body per documentation from EPCR.
Given asystole and PEA findings, we initiate protocol 2-7: cardiac arrest, per standard of care in EPCR documentation.
IO access established in right humerus. First dose of epinephrine administered at 22:35 per EPCR.
AutoPulse CPR device applied. Mechanical compressions initiated after manual hands-off event noted at 22:32 in EPCR.
Second epinephrine dose given via IO at 22:41. No physical or vasopressor response observed at this time, per EPCR.
Pulse notes still 0; resp rate at 12. EKG remains asystolic. No ROSC, as documented in EPCR and confirmed in charting.
Mechanical CPR rate 120/min consistently. Maintaining waveform capnography with ventilations going smoothly at current rpm.
Second epinephrine dose shows no immediate response in physiology with EKG flat. Pulse remains non-palpable.
Normal capnography detected at 12/min. No loss of waveform detected in any segment of transport.
Would you confirm St. Mary's Hospital as the most appropriate receiving facility for this patient's presentation?.
Confirmed. Closest facility suitable for continued resuscitation and care. Suggested at time of refractive hand-off.
Mechanical CPR still active on assigned stretcher. Patient is fully secured for transport per EPCR note at time of hand-off.
Capnography is stable throughout transport with no reductions in waveform. Ventilations per order.
Transport has commenced at 22:38 EPCR time. PEA rhythm noted throughout en route without improvement.
Oxygen saturation remains at zero; EKG rhythm remains asystolic backbone of arrest as recorded in EPCR.
Epinephrine dose number three prepped at 22:53, ready for intraosseous administration per protocol 2-7.
No spontaneous cardiac rhythms detected through interpretation of EKG at time of final dose.
Defibrillator preparation initiated. No rhythm changes detected. Patient remains pulseless.
Any changes in status of IO or ETT?.
IO remains in place. Airway secure with capnography wave maintained at 12/min. No compromise reported en route.
ALS care remains full to transport phase per Protocol 2-7. Current machine mechanical CPR continues accurately until ED hand-off.
Confirmed: ALS care completed in transit. No anticipated cardiac activity predicted upon ED arrival.
Patient transported as pulseless and apneic at all points. ROSC not noted in EPCR per protocol adherence.
DNR mentioned upon intake but no documentation available. ED was pre-notified of possible but no legal copy present at time of intake.
Patient is 95F, found supine and unresponsive at time of first responder arrival with no palpable pulse or spontaneous breaths.
Initial actions: BVM taken to field ventilation and then transition to ETT by EMS. AutoPulse inserted for continuous compressions at time of hand-off.
IO placed in right humeral, two epinephrine doses given. Supported via capnography over waveform for duration of transport.
No return of spontaneous circulation documented in field. Patient continued PEA on cardiac monitor and remained pulseless en route to facility.
DNR status mentioned but not documented. All EMS actions were consistent with ROSP as per agreed protocol.
All medications given via intraosseous access. Capnography was continually confirmed. Final tube secure done with x-collar.
Firefighters aid maintained compressions until mechsystolic systems were activated. ETT remained confirmed all through transport route.
ED aware, bed prepared at room 30. AutoPulse detached on arrival. All steps followed with no complications during transport.
Arrived as deceased at 22:53 according to EPCR -all care had been provided within standard.
Patient confirmed as deceased upon arrival per EPCR documentation. All interventions fully supported as per field directive and protocols.
All patients' belongings or documentation in-hand. No signs of trauma or spillage at arrival. No unusual findings at transport end.
No allergies, medications, or current intake on file that would affect transport or hospital disposition recorded as of arrival.
Primary hypertension only in medical history. All field visits and care steps followed per circumstance with full adherence to guidelines.
No prior complaints reported contrary to distress parameters as of admission. No extraneous event or history at narrator's perception.
Pt arrived as deceased and immediately under facility care. All equipment on note site. Patients were fully prepped and documented in NPCs.
DNR status was not able to be verified at scene. Staff were advised accordingly and no coercion was used to continue resuscitation.
All hand-off logs and EPCRs updated. Standard care followed in agreement with state and facility regulations.
Equipment sanitized and transported for facility review before end of shift. All documentation and patient care reports ready for check-in on site.
All field documentation and patient data reviewed. Units and logs are updated. Transport was a complete and stable experience.
